name: ZZ
description: A method that begins with edge orientation (EOLine), enabling rotationless F2L solving. Popular for one-handed solving and ZBLL finishing.

steps.name: EOLine
steps.description: Orient all edges and place the DF and DB edges, creating a line on the bottom. This ensures all subsequent moves are rotation-free.
tips: Edge orientation detection is the hardest part

steps.name: F2L (ZZF2L)
steps.description: Complete both first two layer blocks using only R, U, and L moves. No rotations needed since edges are already oriented.

steps.name: Last Layer
steps.description: Solve the last layer. With oriented edges, can use ZBLL (493 algs) for one-look, or OCLL+PLL for two-look.

creator: Zbigniew Zborowski
